title: How Often Should You Groom Your Dog? – Breed-by-Breed Guide (2026)
seoTitle: How Often to Groom Your Dog – Breed Guide | GetPawsy
seoDescription: How often should you groom your dog? Breed-by-breed grooming schedule for brushing, bathing, nail trimming and ear cleaning. Vet-backed advice.
excerpt: A breed-by-breed guide to grooming frequency. Learn how often to brush, bathe, trim nails, and clean ears based on your dog's coat type and lifestyle.
keywords: how often to groom a dog
how often to bathe a dog

sections.heading: Grooming Frequency by Coat Type
sections.content:
Your dog's grooming schedule depends primarily on coat type:

**Short Coats (Beagles, Boxers):** Weekly brushing, bath every 6-8 weeks, nails every 2-3 weeks.

**Double Coats (Huskies, Golden Retrievers):** Brush 2-3x per week (daily during shedding season), bath every 6-8 weeks, nails every 2-3 weeks.

**Curly Coats (Poodles, Doodles):** Daily brushing, bath every 4-6 weeks, professional trim every 6-8 weeks, nails every 2-3 weeks.

**Long Coats (Yorkies, Maltese):** Daily brushing, bath every 3-4 weeks, nails every 2-3 weeks.

For the full tool guide, read our [dog grooming tools guide](/guides/dog-grooming-tools-guide). Find the right brush in our [dog brush collection](/collections/best-dog-brushes).

sections.heading: Bathing Schedule
sections.content:
Most dogs only need a bath every 4-8 weeks. Over-bathing strips natural oils and causes dry, flaky skin.

**Exceptions for more frequent bathing:**
- Dogs that swim regularly
- Dogs that roll in dirt/mud frequently
- Dogs with skin conditions (consult your vet)
- Working dogs or highly active outdoor dogs

Always use a pH-balanced dog shampoo. Human shampoo has the wrong pH and damages dog skin.

For complete bathing and grooming routines, read our [grooming tools guide](/guides/dog-grooming-tools-guide). Browse [grooming kits](/collections/best-dog-grooming-kits) for everything you need.

sections.heading: Nail Trimming Schedule
sections.content:
Trim nails every **2-3 weeks** regardless of breed. If you can hear your dog's nails clicking on hard floors, they're overdue.

Overgrown nails cause pain, affect posture, and can lead to joint problems over time. For a step-by-step trimming guide, see our [dog nail trimming guide](/guides/dog-nail-trimming-guide).

Browse nail clippers and grooming tools in our [grooming collection](/collections/best-dog-grooming-kits).

faq.question: Can you groom a dog too much?
faq.answer: You can over-bathe (stripping natural oils) but you can rarely over-brush. Regular brushing distributes oils, catches skin issues, and reduces shedding. Daily brushing is ideal for long and curly coats.

faq.question: Should I groom my dog in winter?
faq.answer: Yes. Winter grooming is important — matted coats lose insulating ability. Keep the regular brushing schedule but reduce bathing frequency. Ensure your dog is fully dry before going outside.

featuredSnippet: Most dogs need brushing 1-3 times per week, bathing every 4-8 weeks, and nail trimming every 2-3 weeks. Curly and long-coated breeds need daily brushing to prevent matting. Short-coated breeds need weekly brushing.
